questions.question: What is the total market value of account ACC-0042?

failure_modes.bronze: Bronze tables (TOPAZ, EMERALD, RUBY) each use a different account identifier format (C-XXXXXX, PORT-XXXX, FND-XXXX) with no canonical mapping. There is no account dimension table in Bronze, so filtering by 'ACC-0042' returns nothing — that identifier format does not exist in any Bronze column.
failure_modes.silver: Silver stores raw source account keys (C-XXXXXX, PORT-XXXX, FND-XXXX). There is no canonical account_id in POSITIONS_INTEGRATED, so filtering by 'ACC-0042' returns no data.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE groups by account_ref, which carries raw source system keys (C-XXXXXX, PORT-XXXX, FND-XXXX) unresolved. Canonical account IDs like 'ACC-0042' do not appear in the model — account-level queries require knowing the source system identifier format for each source.

questions.ground_truth_description: Total market value of account ACC-0042 (George Group Trust)

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    account_ref,
    position_date,
    market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  account_ref,
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date,
  SUM(market_value) AS total_market_value
FROM __positions
WHERE
  account_ref = 'ACC-0042'
GROUP BY
  account_ref
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 341b3418-cc1f-4f91-ad54-564915c6e47f)
;

gold_naive.sql:
WITH __positions_naive AS (
  SELECT
    account_id,
    position_date,
    total_market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
)
SELECT
  account_id,
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date,
  SUM(total_market_value) AS total_market_value
FROM __positions_naive
WHERE
  account_id = 'ACC-0042'
GROUP BY
  account_id
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: d5cf0125-3907-4d6f-a0d8-7e255efb7e48)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    account_id,
    market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
), __account AS (
  SELECT
    account_id,
    account_name
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_account
)
SELECT
  a.account_id,
  a.account_name,
  SUM(p.market_value) AS total_market_value
FROM __position AS p
JOIN __account AS a
  ON p.account_id = a.account_id
WHERE
  a.account_id = 'ACC-0042'
GROUP BY
  a.account_id,
  a.account_name
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 14822863-058e-409c-a84c-aae48fb11f81)
;

questions.question: What is the total quantity of Rasmussen LLC plc held across all accounts?

failure_modes.bronze: Bronze tables use three different security identifier systems (CUSIP from Topaz, ticker from Emerald, ISIN from Ruby) with no shared key and no security name column. Resolving a security by name requires joining all three against a security master — which Bronze does not contain. 30 of 200 securities are absent from BRONZE.SECURITY_MASTER_STUB entirely.
failure_modes.silver: POSITIONS_INTEGRATED has no security_name column — Cortex cannot resolve a name lookup. Even if it filters by identifier, SEC-0018 (RLP) is absent from BRONZE.SECURITY_MASTER_STUB, so security_master_id is NULL and any join-based lookup returns zero rows.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE filters to security_master_id IS NOT NULL at construction time, permanently dropping the 30 unmastered securities (~15% of rows). A lookup for 'Rasmussen LLC plc' (SEC-0018) falls in the excluded set — the model cannot answer the question because the relevant rows were silently removed during the dbt build.

questions.ground_truth_description: Total quantity of Rasmussen LLC plc (ticker: RLP, SEC-0018) across all accounts

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    security_ref,
    position_date,
    quantity
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity,
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date
FROM __positions
WHERE
  security_ref = 'Rasmussen LLC plc'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 9be7e85c-b298-4819-a085-d81a238667b8)
;

gold_naive.sql:
WITH __positions_naive AS (
  SELECT
    security_master_id,
    total_quantity
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
), __securities_naive AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    security_name
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.securities_naive
)
SELECT
  SUM(p.total_quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM __positions_naive AS p
LEFT OUTER JOIN __securities_naive AS s
  ON p.security_master_id = s.security_id
WHERE
  s.security_name = 'Rasmussen LLC plc'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 433fa409-b597-42e5-9661-5b3ab5b9ef0a)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    quantity
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
), __security AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    security_name
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_security
)
SELECT
  SUM(p.quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM __position AS p
JOIN __security AS s
  ON p.security_id = s.security_id
WHERE
  s.security_name = 'Rasmussen LLC plc'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: a7b3b106-d8b2-4210-ab2c-4fa9cb028ee1)
;

questions.question: What is the total market value of all positions?

failure_modes.bronze: Topaz, Emerald, and Ruby each publish market values using a different pricing methodology (custodian EOD, PM evaluated ±0.3%, NAV ±0.5%). Bronze exposes all three independently with no reconciliation layer. A total AUM query against raw Bronze tables must either pick one source or blend three incompatible methodologies — both produce a number that cannot be traced to any system of record.
failure_modes.silver: POSITIONS_INTEGRATED.price blends custodian closing prices (Topaz), PM evaluated prices (Emerald), and NAV-based prices (Ruby). The total is internally consistent but computed from three different pricing methodologies that would never appear together in any system of record.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE aggregates with AVG(price) across all three sources, blending custodian EOD, PM evaluated (±0.3%), and NAV (±0.5%) prices with equal weight. The result is a precise number, but it corresponds to no single authoritative pricing methodology and would fail an investment management audit.

questions.ground_truth_description: Total market value across all 4,886 DW positions (custodian closing price basis)

bronze.sql:
WITH __topaz_positions AS (
  SELECT
    as_of_dt,
    mkt_val
  FROM gsf_demo.bronze.topaz_positions
)
SELECT
  MIN(as_of_dt) AS start_date,
  MAX(as_of_dt) AS end_date,
  SUM(mkt_val) AS total_market_value
FROM __topaz_positions
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: b80c264f-456d-44e3-87e9-3530edca23af)
;

silver.sql:

  SELECT

    market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date,
  SUM(market_value) AS total_market_value
FROM __positions
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 55ecd335-f252-4196-82cc-b2705ceda1ef)
;

gold_naive.sql:

questions.question: What is the total unrealized loss across all positions with a loss greater than $10,000?

failure_modes.bronze: Ruby's Bronze table (RUBY_POSITIONS) has no unrealized G/L column — fund accounting systems track book cost, not mark-to-market G/L. A cross-source filter on unrealized loss silently excludes all 4,886 Ruby positions. Topaz and Emerald do publish G/L, but combining them requires a cross-source join that Bronze does not support.
failure_modes.silver: POSITIONS_INTEGRATED.unrealized_gl is NULL for all Ruby-sourced rows (22% of the table). Any filter on unrealized_gl silently excludes those positions. Additionally, three different column names map to 'unrealized gain/loss' across the source schemas — Cortex may not recognize the concept in all three.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE.total_unrealized_gl is computed as SUM(unrealized_gl). Ruby rows (22% of all positions) have NULL unrealized_gl, which SUM() silently excludes. Any filter on unrealized_gl < -10000 is structurally limited to two of three source systems, with no warning or indicator of the gap.

questions.ground_truth_description: Sum of unrealized_gain_loss for all positions where value < -$10,000

bronze.sql:
WITH __topaz_positions AS (
  SELECT
    unrlzd_gl
  FROM gsf_demo.bronze.topaz_positions
)
SELECT
  SUM(unrlzd_gl) AS total_unrealized_loss
FROM __topaz_positions
WHERE
  unrlzd_gl < -10000
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 83e21801-78f4-4642-9039-f5208ef95651)
;

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    unrealized_gl
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  SUM(unrealized_gl) AS total_unrealized_loss
FROM __positions
WHERE
  unrealized_gl < -10000
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 60402c02-6f25-4fb3-8c56-dde410719ccb)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    unrealized_gain_loss
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
)
SELECT
  SUM(p.unrealized_gain_loss) AS total_unrealized_loss
FROM __position AS p
WHERE
  p.unrealized_gain_loss < -10000
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 9ba36d0c-a100-4f03-af7a-f7e0ddec11ee)
;

questions.question: What is the total quantity of Fixed Income securities held across all portfolios?

failure_modes.bronze: Emerald's Bronze table is lot-level (2-3 rows per position), while Topaz and Ruby are position-level. Bronze provides no grain-normalization layer. Summing quantity across all three sources overcounts Emerald positions by the lot-expansion factor — a Fixed Income quantity query against raw Bronze returns a total inflated by 2-3× for the OMS-sourced positions.
failure_modes.silver: POSITIONS_INTEGRATED contains Emerald lot-level rows (LOT-* record_ids) alongside Topaz/Ruby position-level rows (POS-*, NAV-*). Grain is mixed and invisible — COUNT queries overcount Emerald positions, and any query treating one row as one position returns a lot fragment instead of the full position value. The observable symptom: a list query and a count query for the same question return different cardinalities with no error.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE.total_quantity uses SUM(quantity) without grain normalization. Emerald lot-level rows (2-3 per position) are aggregated alongside Topaz/Ruby position-level rows (1 per position). The result overstates Fixed Income quantity by the average lot-expansion factor for Emerald-sourced securities.

questions.ground_truth_description: Total quantity of Fixed Income securities at position grain (DW_POSITION)

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    position_date,
    quantity
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity,
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date
FROM __positions
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: c9191037-bd5e-4002-9832-9ff0ddf75053)
;

gold_naive.sql:
WITH __positions_naive AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    total_quantity
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
)
SELECT
  SUM(total_quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM __positions_naive
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 6439aed8-42d7-49df-909f-fb4624eec28a)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    quantity
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
), __security AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    security_id
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_security
)
SELECT
  SUM(p.quantity) AS total_quantity_fixed_income
FROM __position AS p
JOIN __security AS s
  ON p.security_id = s.security_id
WHERE
  s.asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 30ee7c45-7054-4ca8-9303-1ae51fcda736)
;

questions.question: How many positions exist as of December 31, 2024?

failure_modes.bronze: The three Bronze tables use three different date columns: AS_OF_DT (Topaz, settlement date), POSITION_DATE (Emerald, trade date), and NAV_DATE (Ruby, NAV strike date). At year-end these conventions can fall on different calendar days. A count 'as of 2024-12-31' requires choosing a date semantics — Bronze exposes all three without indicating which is canonical.
failure_modes.silver: Topaz uses AS_OF_DT (settlement date), Emerald uses positionDate (trade date), and Ruby uses nav_date (NAV strike date). At year-end these dates can fall on different calendar days. The ETL silently chose one convention — the column looks normalized but the semantic distinction between date types is lost.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE maps all three date conventions to a single position_date column. The date alignment logic embeds one convention as authoritative, but the COUNT at year-end will include or exclude edge-case positions depending on whether settlement date, trade date, or NAV date was selected as canonical — and the model does not declare which it used.

questions.ground_truth_description: Count of positions in DW_POSITION with position_date = 2024-12-31 (trade-date basis)

bronze.sql:
WITH __topaz_positions AS (
  SELECT
    as_of_dt
  FROM gsf_demo.bronze.topaz_positions

SELECT
  COUNT(*)
FROM __topaz_positions
WHERE
  as_of_dt = '2024-12-31'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 9338304c-ae74-4c71-a3ec-9ae32f754d00)
;

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    position_date
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*)
FROM __positions
WHERE
  position_date = '2024-12-31'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 3cf063c3-40f6-4656-a81d-b154df47f6ae)
;

gold_naive.sql:

  SELECT
    position_date
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*)
FROM __positions_naive
WHERE
  position_date = '2024-12-31'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 660ec5f0-968c-4483-9be1-35707435d2d9)
;

gold.sql:

  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position

SELECT

FROM __position
WHERE
  position_date = '2024-12-31'

questions.question: What is the total market value of all Fixed Income positions?

failure_modes.bronze: BRONZE.SECURITY_MASTER_STUB is incomplete — 30 of 200 securities are absent. Joining Bronze position tables to this stub to resolve asset_class silently drops those positions from any asset-class-based aggregation. Fixed Income AUM is understated by an unknown amount with no error or warning returned to the caller.
failure_modes.silver: 4 of 27 Fixed Income securities are absent from BRONZE.SECURITY_MASTER_STUB. Any Silver query that joins on security_master_id to retrieve asset_class silently drops those 4 securities' positions, understating Fixed Income AUM by an unknown and unannounced amount.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE filters to security_master_id IS NOT NULL at construction time, permanently excluding ~15% of positions (30 unmastered securities, including 4 Fixed Income names). These rows are gone — no downstream query can recover them. Fixed Income market value is systematically understated with a confident-looking result.

questions.ground_truth_description: Total market value of all Fixed Income positions (DW_POSITION joined to DW_SECURITY)

WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    position_date,
    market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  SUM(market_value) AS total_market_value,
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date
FROM __positions
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 3799e1df-b051-4048-b249-338ace88c4d8)
;

gold_naive.sql:
WITH __positions_naive AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    position_date,
    total_market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
)
SELECT
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date,
  SUM(total_market_value) AS total_market_value
FROM __positions_naive
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 66247d5e-dce5-45a0-8f5a-202ddd9b681c)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
), __security AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    security_id
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_security
)
SELECT
  SUM(p.market_value) AS total_market_value_fixed_income
FROM __position AS p

questions.question: What is the total unrealized gain/loss for the Equity sleeve?

failure_modes.bronze: Topaz, Emerald, and Ruby each compute unrealized G/L using a different cost basis method (custodian cost, specific lot identification, and book cost respectively). Bronze exposes all three in separate tables with no reconciliation. A cross-source sum of unrealized gains mixes three incompatible accounting methods in a single number.
failure_modes.silver: POSITIONS_INTEGRATED.cost_basis blends three incompatible accounting methods: Emerald specific lot identification, Topaz custodian cost, and Ruby book cost. The computed unrealized gain is internally consistent but cannot be reconciled to any system of record and would fail an audit.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE.total_unrealized_gl aggregates G/L from three sources using incompatible cost basis methods: Emerald (specific lot), Topaz (custodian cost), Ruby (book cost). The sum is a single precise number, but it mixes three accounting standards. Additionally, Ruby rows (22% of positions) are excluded entirely due to NULL unrealized_gl.

questions.ground_truth_description: Sum of unrealized_gain_loss for Equity positions (specific identification cost basis)

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    position_date,
    unrealized_gl
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  SUM(unrealized_gl) AS total_unrealized_gl,
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date
FROM __positions
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Equity'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 1b9128ce-72af-4e0a-bcab-bcc411eb5869)
;

gold_naive.sql:
WITH __positions_naive AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    total_unrealized_gl
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
)
SELECT
  SUM(total_unrealized_gl) AS total_unrealized_gl
FROM __positions_naive
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Equity'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 29d7a099-7a8e-42b0-831a-f8ef3db9e78e)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    unrealized_gain_loss
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
), __security AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    security_id
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_security
)
SELECT
  SUM(p.unrealized_gain_loss) AS total_unrealized_gain_loss_equity
FROM __position AS p
JOIN __security AS s
  ON p.security_id = s.security_id
WHERE
  s.asset_class = 'Equity'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: de814bca-4fe1-4eb6-8298-06579bced8c4)
;

questions.question: What percentage of total AUM is allocated to Fixed Income?

failure_modes.bronze: Asset class is derivable only by joining position tables to BRONZE.SECURITY_MASTER_STUB. With 30 unmastered securities absent from the stub, ~15% of positions have no resolvable asset_class. Both the Fixed Income numerator and the total AUM denominator exclude those positions — making every percentage allocation systematically biased from an incomplete base.
failure_modes.silver: ~15% of positions in POSITIONS_INTEGRATED have NULL asset_class (unmastered securities). The percentage is computed on a partial AUM base — both the Fixed Income numerator and the total denominator exclude unmastered positions, making every sleeve percentage systematically wrong with no indication of the gap.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE.asset_class is NULL for the ~15% of positions where security_master_id is NULL (filtered out at construction). The Fixed Income percentage is computed on a partial AUM base — both the numerator and denominator exclude unmastered securities. The result is plausible and precise but provably wrong.

questions.ground_truth_description: Fixed Income market value / total market value x 100 (complete DW universe)

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN asset_class = 'Fixed Income' THEN market_value ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(market_value), 0) * 100 AS fixed_income_pct
FROM __positions
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 2fe0ce3a-945a-4157-98a0-e2423d13da2a)
;

gold_naive.sql:
WITH __positions_naive AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    total_market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
)
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN asset_class = 'Fixed Income' THEN total_market_value ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(total_market_value), 0) AS fixed_income_pct
FROM __positions_naive
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: b7873552-510f-40aa-a52e-757d98c3d6a9)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    market_value
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
), __security AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    security_id
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_security
)
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN s.asset_class = 'Fixed Income' THEN p.market_value ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(NULLIF(SUM(p.market_value), 0), 0) * 100 AS fixed_income_pct_of_aum
FROM __position AS p
JOIN __security AS s
  ON p.security_id = s.security_id
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 4c0aed0d-cf37-4a81-8d3f-7643aeae0d53)
;

questions.question: How many positions have unrealized losses greater than $10,000?

failure_modes.bronze: Ruby's Bronze table has no unrealized_gl column — it tracks book cost but not mark-to-market G/L. A count of positions with unrealized loss below -$10,000 can only be answered from Topaz and Emerald, covering 2 of 3 source systems. Bronze is silent about the 4,886 Ruby fund positions with no error returned.
failure_modes.silver: Ruby is a fund accounting system — it does not publish unrealized gain/loss. All 4,886 Ruby-sourced rows in POSITIONS_INTEGRATED have unrealized_gl = NULL. A filter WHERE unrealized_gl < -10000 silently excludes 22% of the portfolio. The result looks complete but covers only two of three source systems.
failure_modes.gold_naive: POSITIONS_NAIVE filters out Ruby rows (22% of all positions) because SUM(unrealized_gl) evaluates to NULL for them. A count of positions with unrealized loss < -$10,000 is structurally limited to Topaz + Emerald — the Ruby fund accounting system's contribution is permanently excluded from this query.

gold.sql:

;

questions.question: What is the total cost basis for all Fixed Income positions?

failure_modes.bronze: Three ambiguities compound: (A1) security identifiers are fragmented across CUSIP, ticker, and ISIN with no shared key; (A4) account identifiers use source system keys, not canonical IDs; (A9) cost_basis differs by source — Emerald uses specific lot cost, Topaz uses custodian cost, Ruby uses book cost. A cross-source Fixed Income cost basis query must resolve all three before producing a meaningful number.
failure_modes.silver: Compounds three ambiguities: (A1) security identifiers are fragmented across CUSIP/ticker/ISIN with no shared key; (A4) account identifiers use source keys not canonical account_id; (A9) cost_basis blends lot identification, custodian cost, and book cost methods. The Silver answer is both incomplete and methodologically unsound.
failure_modes.gold_naive: Three ambiguities compound in POSITIONS_NAIVE: (A1) security joins require security_master_id, silently dropping 15% of positions (including some Fixed Income names); (A4) account_ref is a raw source key, making account-scoped cost basis queries unreachable; (A9) total_cost_basis mixes specific lot cost (Emerald), custodian cost (Topaz), and book cost (Ruby) — three incompatible accounting methods in a single SUM.

questions.ground_truth_description: Sum of cost_basis for Fixed Income positions (specific identification, DW grain)

silver.sql:
WITH __positions AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    position_date,
    cost_basis
  FROM gsf_demo.silver.positions_integrated
)
SELECT
  SUM(cost_basis) AS total_cost_basis,
  MIN(position_date) AS start_date,
  MAX(position_date) AS end_date
FROM __positions
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: ca0069d8-9d62-45ff-9a2c-91b88c762634)
;

gold_naive.sql:
WITH __positions_naive AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    total_cost_basis
  FROM gsf_demo.gold_naive.positions_naive
)
SELECT
  SUM(total_cost_basis) AS total_cost_basis
FROM __positions_naive
WHERE
  asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 9d6ad7fb-e6e1-48e0-a2b2-1a2213a79afc)
;

gold.sql:
WITH __position AS (
  SELECT
    security_id,
    cost_basis
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_position
), __security AS (
  SELECT
    asset_class,
    security_id
  FROM gsf_demo.gold.dw_security
)
SELECT
  SUM(p.cost_basis) AS total_cost_basis_fixed_income
FROM __position AS p
JOIN __security AS s
  ON p.security_id = s.security_id
WHERE
  s.asset_class = 'Fixed Income'
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: 7942b6f2-05d8-4a3e-b78d-5c43b0b32e8b)
;
